items.title: StrictRestoreRunner BREAKDOWN_INVARIANT_VIOLATION
items.root_cause: CR-6
items.log_evidence_excerpt: BREAKDOWN_INVARIANT_VIOLATION occurrences in recoverland_debug.log over 2026-05-13..2026-05-15 window: 0 (was non-zero before f4a9932). _commit_strict folds skipped_idempotent into _total_ok; _rollback_strict and commit-failure branches credit _failed_other while discarding stale per-group accumulators.

history.evidence: Live recoverland_debug.log shows BREAKDOWN_INVARIANT_VIOLATION WARNING during temporal restore: bucket_sum diverges from _total_ok+_total_fail. Three causes: (A) skipped_idempotent events not counted in _total_ok, (B) _rollback_strict adds unprocessed actions to _failed_other twice, (C) _commit_strict failed branch double-credits failed_other.

restore_runner.py: per-group accumulators (_strict_group_*) reset at _begin_strict_for_layer. _commit_strict credits applied/skipped/failed_* from group buckets only on commit success; _total_ok += ok_count + skipped_count. _rollback_strict preserves the triggering reason (target_absent/geometry_drift) and credits the rest to _failed_other. _commit_strict failed branch resets buckets and credits whole layer to _failed_other.

User ran chained rewinds and emitted full audit log 2026-05-13..2026-05-15. Grep BREAKDOWN_INVARIANT_VIOLATION on recoverland_debug.log over this window: 0 hits. Invariant holds across the 4 distinct rewinds observed (trace_ids 47432dbd, 88d227a2, b0783f7a, plus the chained second pass).

items.title: StrictRestoreRunner ne doit pas rollback toute la couche sur soft fail isole
items.root_cause: CR-2
items.log_evidence_excerpt: Live log eid=42768 (UPDATE comp on cables fid=464) returns target_absent because user deleted the feature OUTSIDE the rewind scope (no INSERT comp planned, no entry in fid_remap). StrictRestoreRunner used to call _rollback_strict, discarding the 31 other valid compensations on cables. Patched to isolate soft fails and continue the chunk.

history.evidence: User report: second rewind 'leaves traces'. Live log shows eid=42768 UPDATE comp on cables fid=464 -> target_absent -> _rollback_strict for the whole layer. 31 already-applied compensations on cables are wiped out by the rollback. Chained rewinds therefore leave residual edits because each rewind hits the same target_absent path and rolls back the entire layer batch.

Static trace of plan_temporal_restore + log analysis: planner produces UPDATE comp for eid=42768 fid=464 even though no INSERT comp covers fid=464 (the user DELETE for fid=464 is outside the rewind scope, so the post-rewind expected state for fid=464 = absent). _buffer_update detects target=None and returns FAILED reason_code=target_absent. _process_strict_chunk_inner treats this as a hard failure and calls _rollback_strict, wiping the 31 other applied compensations.

restore_runner.py _process_strict_chunk_inner: introduce soft_fail = reason in (target_absent, geometry_drift). For soft fails the chunk increments the relevant bucket and continues with `continue` instead of calling _rollback_strict. items.title: ReView Snapshot 'as-of T' incomplet â€” features non tracees invisibles
items.root_cause: CR-RV-1: reconstruct_snapshot_at ne reconstruit que les entites ayant au moins 1 evenement <= T. Toute feature presente a T mais jamais tracee (donnee de base anterieure au tracking, ou jamais editee) tombe dans n_unknown et reste invisible dans l'overlay (core/temporal_snapshot_engine.py:110-121). Resultat: le passe affiche ne montre que ce qui a change, pas la majorite non modifiee egalement presente a T.

history.evidence: Mandat user (doute sur le mode ReView avant publication) + plan 'RecoverLand team and ReView'. Le mode Snapshot melange l'etat reconstruit des features modifiees et devrait aussi montrer les features inchangees presentes a T.

CONTRAT propose pour ReView Snapshot. Etat a T, par feature source: (a) historique trace -> replay jusqu'a T (geom + attrs); (b) aucun historique -> etat courant present a T (presume stable, car non edite); (c) feature tracee creee apres T (premier evenement > T) -> masquee (deja gere via n_unknown); (d) feature dont le dernier evenement <= T est DELETE -> masquee (n_absent). Invariant: non-destructif (couches sources jamais modifiees), overlays memoire uniquement. Effets de bord: lecture de la couche source pour les features non tracees. Hors perimetre: sous-modes Diff et Time Lens. RISQUES (a arbitrer): R-1 MUST architecture du merge baseline (3 options ci-dessous). R-2 MUST perf: ne pas relire la couche source entiere a chaque changement de date (deleguer /perf). R-3 MUST exactitude: une feature non tracee supprimee hors QGIS avant T serait montree a tort (deleguer /antithese). R-4 SHOULD compat wkbType/geometryType + Multi* (deleguer /qgis-compat + /qgis-plugin). OPTIONS: Opt1 fusion baseline au rendu â€” SnapshotOverlaySession peint aussi les features live dont l'entite n'a aucun evenement (ou 1er evenement > T); l'engine renvoie l'ensemble des entity_key tracees par datasource pour soustraction. Opt2 capture d'un baseline initial au demarrage du tracking (chaque feature obtient un INSERT-equivalent) â€” plus correct, plus lourd, migration a prevoir. Opt3 couche source live sous l'overlay reconstruit avec distinction visuelle â€” moins cher, mais pas un vrai as-of-T. ANTITHESES a attaquer en G3.5: FID recycle dans la meme seconde; edition de la couche source hors QGIS (etat live != etat a T); feature non tracee supprimee avant T affichee a tort; cout O(features x changements de date) si relecture complete; mismatch CRS/wkbType; geometries Multi rejetees silencieusement; explosion memoire sur 20+ couches volumineuses. VERDICT: GO_WITH_NOTES â€” privilegier Opt1 sous reserve du chiffrage /perf (cache des cles non tracees, lecture unique au demarrage de session) et de l'antithese R-3; Opt2 reste l'option correcte long terme. Implementation = item suivant, ce cadrage ne touche aucun code.
